# FINALIZE_GOVERNED_PRE_EXECUTION_STACK_V1

## Status

Frozen and certified.

This milestone certifies the full constitutional pre-execution governance stack:

`Human -> Browser Companion -> Intent Interpretation -> Codex Task Synthesis -> Codex Handoff Package -> Execution Authorization Gate`

## Certified Included Components

1. Browser Companion
2. Local Preview Runtime
3. Intent Interpretation Layer
4. Codex Task Synthesis Layer
5. Codex Handoff Package Layer
6. Execution Authorization Gate
7. Replay Identity Chain
8. Explicit Human Approval Model
9. Revocation Support
10. Temporary Authority Tokens

## Constitutional Boundary

The system may authorize bounded downstream execution eligibility, but does not execute downstream tasks.

## Certified Exclusions

- Codex execution
- automatic prompt dispatch
- downstream task execution
- orchestration
- retries/fallbacks
- hidden continuation
- autonomous loops
- worker coordination
- unrestricted subprocess execution
- `shell=True`
- hidden networking
- automatic tool routing
- self-modifying governance
- persistent execution authority

## Closure Statement

The governed pre-execution stack is closed and frozen. Execution remains
constitutionally external until a future, separately governed consumer proves
admissibility against this baseline.
